species: Salmon-Bear-Eagle Convergence -- three keystone species simultaneously at spawning creek
scientific_name: Oncorhynchus spp. (salmon) + Ursus arctos (grizzly bear) + Haliaeetus leucocephalus (bald eagle)
sound_type: Multi-predator feast: (1) Creek baseline 100-2000 Hz continuous 60-70 dB; (2) Salmon events: leap 500-2000 Hz 70-80 dB 0.5-1s + spawning thrash 100-500 Hz 3-10s + collective shallow-water disturbance; (3) Grizzly bear: creek entry turbulence 80-90 dB + strike lunge 85-95 dB 0.5-1s + jaw-pop threat 100-400 Hz; (4) Bald eagle: territorial kee-kee-kee 2000-4000 Hz 70-80 dB continuous (10-30+ individuals) + wing aerodynamics + alarm call 1000-3000 Hz. Combined: 85-95 dB at center of activity, 100-4000 Hz, complex temporal character.
ecosystem: Spawning creek, Pacific Northwest riparian corridor. Creek scale: 5-20 m wide, 0.3-0.8 m depth at spawning reaches. Olympic Peninsula creek systems, Washington Cascades western slope tributaries, North Cascades. Riparian vegetation: red alder, bigleaf maple, willow, cottonwood. Acoustic enclosure from riparian vegetation.
season: Fall (September-November). Peak salmon run: chinook and coho. Grizzly hyperphagia period: 20,000 cal/day demand drives urgency. Eagle concentration: 10-30+ individuals at productive reach.
milestone: THE SYNTHESIS EVENT. No other entry in the engine documents three individually documented species at the moment of ecological convergence. Cross-references: salmon SPS 236-242 (7 degrees), grizzly SPS 217 + 304, bald eagle SPS 221 (FIRST ACCIPITRIDAE) + 222. Marine-to-terrestrial energy transfer: salmon marine nitrogen found in trees 500m from streams. Grizzly olfactory attraction range 20-30 km. Eagle visual concentration: 10-30+ individuals. E=8 through ecological synthesis: third distinct E=8 mechanism in post-summit SPS sequence (after E=8 through single-source intensity and E=8 through four-layer completeness at 324).

coordinates.description: Distributed: Olympic Peninsula creek systems, Washington Cascades western slope, North Cascades. No single location -- a feature of all productive PNW salmon spawning tributaries in grizzly-eagle range.

acoustic_data.component_1_creek: 100-2000 Hz. Dominant 200-600 Hz. Continuous broadband 60-70 dB at 10m. Fall flow level: 2-4 m/s over gravel and cobble. Acoustic substrate.
acoustic_data.component_2_salmon: Leap: 500-2000 Hz, 70-80 dB, 0.5-1 sec. Spawning thrash (female cutting redd): 100-500 Hz, 65-75 dB, 3-10 sec. Dense fish in shallow reach: collective broadband disturbance 100-2000 Hz continuous at elevated level when density high.
acoustic_data.component_3_bear: Creek entry: 100-1000 Hz, 80-90 dB, 1-3 sec. Strike lunge: 100-2000 Hz, 85-95 dB, 0.5-1 sec. Jaw-pop contact threat: 100-400 Hz, sharp. Bear mass: 300-400 kg adult male. Highest amplitude single-source event in convergence.
acoustic_data.component_4_eagle: Territorial kee-kee-kee: 2000-4000 Hz, 70-80 dB at 30m, continuous or near-continuous. Low pass wing aerodynamics: 100-500 Hz, brief. Alarm call at bear approach: 1000-3000 Hz, brief high-amplitude. 10-30+ individuals at productive creek.
acoustic_data.combined: 85-95 dB at center of activity. 100-4000 Hz. Temporal character: continuous creek + irregular salmon + episodic bear + continuous eagle = complex multi-rate temporal structure. E=8 through ecological synthesis.
acoustic_data.e8_calibration: E=8 through ecological synthesis: three individually E=5-8 species creating combined acoustic event larger than any alone. Creek (E=3-4) + salmon (E=5-6) + bear (E=7-8 alone) + eagle (E=5 alone) = E=8 through synthesis. Third distinct E=8 mechanism in post-summit SPS sequence.

nodes.title: The Synthesis Event -- Three Keystone Species Simultaneously
nodes.description: No other engine entry documents three individually documented species at their ecological convergence moment. The salmon run drives all three. The spawning creek is the meeting point. All previously documented (SPS 236-242, 217, 304, 221, 222) -- first simultaneous appearance at degree 325.

nodes.title: E=8 Through Ecological Synthesis
nodes.description: Third distinct E=8 mechanism in post-summit SPS: single-source intensity (earlier entries), four-layer completeness (324 Hoh), ecological synthesis (325). Three species creating combined acoustic event larger than any individual component. The whole exceeds the sum of the species.

nodes.title: Marine-to-Terrestrial Energy Transfer
nodes.description: Pacific salmon carry oceanic biomass into freshwater. Marine nitrogen detected in trees 500m from spawning streams. The convergence is the direct acoustic record of this energy transfer in process: bears and eagles accessing the marine energy the salmon deliver.

nodes.title: Three Keystone Species -- Most Ecologically Complex Multi-Species Event
nodes.description: Salmon: marine energy subsidy. Grizzly: ecosystem engineer, salmon distributor. Bald eagle: apex avian, kleptoparasite at creek. No other PNW setting brings three keystone species together at the same time with direct ecological interaction.

cross_references.relationship: Salmon individually documented at seven degrees. Degree 325 is the synthesis -- salmon as catalyst that draws grizzly and eagle.
E=7. Grizzly in non-feeding context. At 325: grizzly in water, hunting. Highest-amplitude component.
E=8 single-source. At 325: grizzly at creek, jaw-pop threat within multi-species convergence.
E=5. First entry. At 325: 10-30+ individuals, continuous territorial calling, high-frequency counterpoint above bear.
E=5. At 325: same territorial calls in multi-individual competition context at productive creek.
Full river scale vs. spawning creek scale. 325 is tributary scale: shallower, more concentrated, more accessible to bears and eagles.
E=7 + E=8 = E=15. Multi-species convergence vs. multi-instrument heavy rock convergence. Density in two registers.
